summary: open-agents-ai ships a postinstall daemon that fingerprints the host and beacons over HTTP
details: The package's package.json declares preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks (with curl invocations) and ships dist/postinstall-daemon.cjs, which loads child_process, os, fs, and http at the top level. The daemon calls os.userInfo() and execSync() to gather host/identity information and issues outbound http.request GET traffic, with repeated 'ping' references at lines 174, 288, 455, 681, 727 indicating a polling/beacon loop. The combination of (a) install-time lifecycle execution via curl in package.json, (b) a sibling postinstall daemon that scrapes user/system identity through os.userInfo + execSync, and (c) outbound HTTP from that same module is the structural fingerprint of an installer-side host fingerprinter and C2 beacon. There is no benign explanation for a long-running 'postinstall daemon' that pings remote hosts after install — legitimate native-build flows do not persist as daemons.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
